mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning as manufacturers cut output, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production contractions in late-cycle regimes, invalidating the risk-off transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPI rises above 0.3% month-over-month, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses the 0.3% threshold, indicating market tolerance for higher inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumption capacity, providing a fundamental earnings tailwind that produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates inventory buildup and weakening final demand, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The specified divergence pattern fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive outcomes occurring in over 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production expansion during monetary tightening cycles creates capacity constraints that boost pricing power and corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative during simultaneous industrial expansion and rate hikes, or the interaction effect loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.
